rationale: Assigns executive accountability and budget for AI which fulfills the Singapore requirement for a clear governance mandate and traceable human leadership.
Establishes cross-functional oversight bodies to ensure diverse perspectives like Legal and Risk are involved in AI decisioning as recommended.
Defines the Kill Switch and intervention authority to bridge the gap between general management and the operational ability to stop an unsafe system.
Mandates documentation of the Record of Decision when balancing performance versus ethics providing the audit trail for model selection.
Requires the use of Model Cards and AI Fact Sheets to provide specific technical disclosures to stakeholders beyond general transparency.
Creates an authoritative registry of all AI assets to prevent Shadow AI and ensure every system is mapped to a specific risk tier and owner.
Evaluates rights and safety risks pre-deployment to determine the appropriate human-in-the-loop oversight level required by the framework.
Requires data provenance and sensitivity labeling to satisfy Singapore requirements for data quality and ensure training data is legally sourced.
Implements quantitative fairness metrics like Disparate Impact Ratio to transform the Fairness principle into a measurable technical unit test.
Mandates audience-appropriate interpretability techniques like SHAP to ensure model logic is explainable to individuals impacted by decisions.
Embeds TEVV into the development lifecycle to ensure that the Singapore principles are engineering constraints rather than afterthoughts.
Ensures updates to models or data undergo a risk review to prevent drift from introducing new ethical or security vulnerabilities after launch.
Establishes normal activity profiles for models to allow for the detection of subtle ethical or performance shifts indicating degradation.
Provides real-time oversight of model performance to satisfy the Singapore requirement for ongoing algorithm management and operational integrity.
Maintains a registry of jurisdictional reporting thresholds to ensure any AI failures are communicated to regulators within legal timeframes.
Provides feedback loops for impacted communities to identify and mitigate unintended consequences not caught during initial testing.
